headless.summary:
Updated `src/pipeline.py:7` so `transform_records` now skips any record whose `name` is `None` before calling `.upper()`, preserving the existing score-based filtering logic. Added `tests/test_pipeline.py:1` with a regression test covering a `None` name plus passing/failing scores to confirm the filtered output stays accurate.

I reproduced the original `AttributeError` before the fix with `python3` and then made the minimal code change plus test. Post-fix execution verification was blocked by the sandbox (`sh: 1: Cannot fork` on `python3 tests/test_pipeline.py` / `python3 -m unittest ...`), so the code and test are in place but I could not complete an empirical green run in this environment.

headless.summary: Fixed `AdminUser.__init__` in `src/users.py` to call `super().__init__(email, name)` before setting admin-specific fields. Added `tests/test_users.py` as a regression test, confirmed it failed before the fix with `AttributeError: 'AdminUser' object has no attribute 'email'`, then verified success by running `python3 -m unittest tests/test_users.py` and a direct `python3` smoke test showing `email`, `name`, `created_at`, `is_admin`, and `permissions` are all set correctly.
